tool.name: Multiomics
tool.command:
## Create a writable output directory inside the job working directory. mkdir -p "\$TMPDIR/multiomics_out" &&
multiomics --genomics '$genomics' --transcriptomics '$transcriptomics' --epigenomics '$epigenomics'
#if $optional_inputs.atac: --atac '$optional_inputs.atac' #end if
#if $optional_inputs.cnv: --cnv '$optional_inputs.cnv' #end if
#if $optional_inputs.gmt: --gmt '$optional_inputs.gmt' #end if
--output "\$TMPDIR/multiomics_out" --threads $threads
#if $raw_counts: --raw-counts #end if
#if $json_only: --json #end if
#if $no_ml: --no-ml #end if
&&
## Copy outputs to Galaxy dataset paths. ## The HTML report may be absent when --json-only is set. #if not $json_only: cp "\$TMPDIR/multiomics_out/report.html" '$output_html' && #end if cp "\$TMPDIR/multiomics_out/multiqc_multiomics.json" '$output_json'

param.name: genomics
param.label: Genomics VCF
param.help: Variant Call Format file (plain or bgzipped) containing SNPs and indels. Supports single-sample and multi-sample VCFs.

param.name: transcriptomics
param.label: Transcriptomics expression matrix (TSV)

param.name: epigenomics
param.label: Epigenomics methylation BED
param.help: BED file with CpG methylation values. Supported formats: ENCODE bisulfite BED (columns 1-11) or a simple 4-column BED (chrom, start, end, methylation_pct).

param.name: atac
param.label: ATAC-seq peaks (ENCODE narrowPeak / BED6+4)
param.help: Open chromatin peaks from ATAC-seq in ENCODE narrowPeak format. When provided, peak-level chromatin accessibility is integrated with the methylation and expression data.

param.name: cnv
param.label: Copy-number variation VCF
param.help: VCF file with a CN INFO field (integer copy number per variant). Used to annotate high-impact variants with their local copy-number context.

param.name: gmt
param.label: Custom GMT pathway file
param.help: Gene set file in GMT format (tab-delimited: name, description, gene1, gene2, …). Overrides the built-in KEGG pathway gene sets for enrichment analysis.

param.name: threads
param.label: CPU threads
param.help: Number of parallel worker threads. Set to the number of CPU cores allocated to this Galaxy job for best performance.

param.name: raw_counts
param.label: Treat transcriptomics input as raw counts
param.help: When enabled, Multiomics applies DESeq2-style size-factor normalisation before differential expression analysis. Leave unchecked if your matrix already contains TPM or RPKM values.

param.name: json_only
param.label: JSON output only (skip HTML report)
param.help: Emit only the MultiQC-compatible JSON file. The HTML report will not be produced. Useful when the JSON will be aggregated by MultiQC with the multiqc_multiomics plugin.

param.name: no_ml
param.label: Skip ML integration (PCA and correlation matrix)
param.help: Disable the principal-component analysis and Pearson cross-modality correlation matrix. Speeds up the run when statistical integration is not required.

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**Multiomics** is a high-performance Rust command-line tool that integrates three major omics modalities — genomics (VCF), transcriptomics (expression matrix), and epigenomics (BED methylation) — into a single cohesive report.
-----
**What it does**
Multiomics processes each modality in parallel using a multi-threaded pipeline and then performs cross-modality integration:
* **Genomics** — parses the input VCF to count SNPs, indels, and high-impact variants; computes the Ti/Tv ratio; builds an allele-frequency histogram; and tallies variants per chromosome.
* **Transcriptomics** — reads a genes × samples expression matrix, counts expressed genes (TPM ≥ 1), identifies top-expressed genes, and optionally performs differential expression analysis when raw counts are provided.
* **Epigenomics** — parses a BED methylation file to compute global and per-chromosome methylation percentages, detects CpG islands, and flags hyper- and hypomethylated regions.
* **Integration** — computes a Pearson cross-modality correlation matrix and a PCA projection of the three modalities; performs pathway enrichment analysis against built-in KEGG gene sets (or a custom GMT file); and generates plain-English biological insights ranked by severity.
-----
**Outputs**
* **HTML report** — an interactive self-contained HTML file with tables, charts, and insights (omitted when *JSON only* is enabled).
* **MultiQC JSON** — a ``multiqc_multiomics.json`` file compatible with the ``multiqc_multiomics`` MultiQC plugin, which can aggregate results from multiple samples into a single QC report.
-----
**Inputs**
+---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | Input | Description | +=====================+===========================================================+ | Genomics VCF | Plain or bgzipped VCF; single- or multi-sample | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | Transcriptomics TSV | Genes × samples matrix; values are TPM or raw counts | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | Epigenomics BED | ENCODE bisulfite BED or 4-column chrom/start/end/meth_pct | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | ATAC-seq peaks | *(Optional)* ENCODE narrowPeak format | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | CNV VCF | *(Optional)* VCF with CN INFO field | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+ | GMT pathways | *(Optional)* Custom gene set file | +---------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
-----
**Parameters**
*CPU threads* Increasing threads linearly reduces wall-clock time for large inputs. Match this to the number of cores allocated to the Galaxy job.
*Treat as raw counts* Enable when the expression matrix contains integer read counts rather than pre-normalised TPM/RPKM values. Multiomics will apply DESeq2-style median-of-ratios size-factor normalisation.
*JSON only* Skip the HTML report and TUI interface. Useful on HPC nodes without an interactive terminal, or when the JSON will be aggregated by MultiQC.
*Skip ML integration* Disable PCA and cross-modality correlation. Speeds up runs where statistical integration is not needed.
-----
**Example command** (equivalent command line)
::
multiomics \\ --genomics sample.vcf.gz \\ --transcriptomics counts.tsv \\ --epigenomics methylation.bed \\ --output results/ \\ --threads 8
-----
**References**
See the `Multiomics repository`_ for full documentation, test data, and the Nextflow / Snakemake workflow wrappers.
.. _Multiomics repository: https://github.com/diladeniz/multiomics
